section_name: I. Compliance with duties of information
content_to_be_reported:
I. Compliance with duties of information
II. Summary 
Part A: Information about the offeror or the person seeking admission to trading 
Part B: Information about the issuer, if different from the offeror or person seeking admission to trading 
Part C: Information about the operator of the trading platform in cases where it draws up the crypto-asset white paper and information about other persons drawing the crypto-asset white paper pursuant to Article 6(1), second subparagraph, of Regulation (EU) 2023/1114 
Part D: Information about the crypto-asset project
Part E: Information about the offer to the public of crypto-assets or their admission to trading
Part F: Information about the crypto-assets 
Part G: Information on the rights and obligations attached to the crypto-assets 
Part H: Information on the underlying technology 
Part I: Information on the risks 
Part J: Information on the sustainability indicators in relation to adverse impact on the climate and other environment-related adverse impacts

section_name: I. Compliance with duties of information
content_to_be_reported: dateOfNotification

field: Statement in accordance with Article 6(3) of Regulation (EU) 2023/1114
section_name: I. Compliance with duties of information

field: Compliance statement in accordance with Article 6(6) of Regulation (EU) 2023/1114
section_name: I. Compliance with duties of information
content_to_be_reported: This crypto-asset white paper complies with Title II of Regulation (EU) 2023/1114 of the European Parliament and of the Council and, to the best of the knowledge of the management body, the information presented in the crypto-asset white paper is fair, clear and not misleading and the crypto-asset white paper makes no omission likely to affect its import.

field: Statement in accordance with Article 6(5), points (a), (b), (c), of Regulation (EU) 2023/1114
section_name: I. Compliance with duties of information
content_to_be_reported: The crypto-asset referred to in this crypto-asset white paper may lose its value in part or in full, may not always be transferable and may not be liquid.

field: Statement in accordance with Article 6(5), point (d), of Regulation (EU) 2023/1114
section_name: I. Compliance with duties of information
content_to_be_reported: If the value of tokenClassification is OTH_UTILITY then write 'The utility token referenced in this white paper may not be redeemable for the goods or services described herein, particularly in the event of project failure or discontinuation of the crypto-asset initiative.' \nElse, write FALSE

field: Statement in accordance with Article 6(5), points (e) and (f), of Regulation (EU) 2023/1114
section_name: I. Compliance with duties of information
content_to_be_reported: The crypto-asset referred to in this white paper is not covered by the investor compensation schemes under Directive 97/9/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council or the deposit guarantee schemes under Directive 2014/49/EU of the European Parliament and of the Council.

field: Warning in accordance with Article 6(7), second subparagraph, of Regulation (EU) 2023/1114
section_name: II. Summary
content_to_be_reported:
Warning
This summary should be read as an introduction to the crypto-asset white paper. The prospective holder should base any decision to purchase this crypto-asset on the content of the crypto-asset white paper as a whole and not on the summary alone. The offer to the public of this crypto-asset does not constitute an offer or solicitation to purchase financial instruments and any such offer or solicitation can be made only by means of a prospectus or other documents pursuant to the applicable national law.

section_name: II. Summary
content_to_be_reported:
A brief, clear and non-technical description of the characteristics of the crypto asset including information about rights and obligations of the purchaser, procedure and conditions for the exercise of those rights and conditions, if any, under which these rights and obligations may be modified. ● Consensus Mechanism: Describe the consensus
mechanism used by the crypto asset.
○ Example: ""Hedera utilises a novel
hashgraph consensus mechanism that
supports high-throughput, low-latency,
low-cost transactions.”
● Rights and Obligations: Outline the rights and
obligations of the purchaser, including how these
can be exercised and any conditions or
procedures.
○ Example: ""HBAR holders can use the
tokens to pay for network services and to
stake them to network nodes for
consensus validation.”
● Modification Conditions: Specify any conditions
under which the rights and obligations of the
crypto asset can be modified.
○ Example: ""Conditions for modifying rights
and obligations are governed by the
Hedera network protocols and community
governance."" ● Utility Token Information: For utility tokens,
provide details about the goods or services
accessible through the tokens and any restrictions
on transferability.
○ Example: ""HBAR tokens provide access to
various Hedera services, such as the
Token Service, Consensus Service, and
Smart Contract Service. Transferability
may be restricted based on network rules
and regulatory requirements."" ● Security and Staking: Describe how the crypto
asset contributes to network security and any
staking mechanisms. ○ Example: ""HBAR are used to secure the
network through staking, which involves
OTH
locking tokens to nodes to achieve a
weighted influence on consensus.

field: Quality and quantity of goods/services linked to utility tokens
section_name: II. Summary
content_to_be_reported: If 05 is FALSE write N/A. If the token classification is Oth_NON_UTILITY (doesnt concern utility tokens) This is N/A. Else, you should provide a summary of fields G.7 and G.11, if they are applicable resembling this format.: Access to Goods and Services:

field: Key information about the offer to the public or admission to trading
section_name: II. Summary
content_to_be_reported:
A brief and non-technical description of the offer to the public including information about the amount of the offer, including, where applicable, any minimum and maximum target subscription goals, issue price of the crypto-asset and subscription fees, the total number of crypto-assets to be offered; prospective holders; description, where applicable, of the various phases of the offer to the public of crypto-assets, including information on discounted purchase price for early purchasers of crypto-assets and subscription period. nWhen applicable, the name of the crypto-asset service provider in charge of the placing of crypto-assets and the form of such placement (with or without a firm commitment basis); nWhen applicable, a brief and non-technical description of the admission to trading, including the name of the trading platform for which the admission is sought.
Content
● Minimum and maximum subscription goals
● Target subscription goals
● Issue price of the crypto-asset
● Subscription fees
● Total number of crypto-assets to be offered
● Prospective holders
● Description of the various phases of the offer to
the public of crypto-assets, including information
on discounted purchase price for early purchasers
of crypto-assets
● Subscription period
● Name of the crypto-asset service provider in
charge of the placing of crypto-assets and the
form of such placement (with or without a firm
commitment basis), when applicable
● Name of the trading platform for which the
admission is sought, when applicable

field: Financial condition for the past three years
section_name: Part A Information about the offeror or the person seeking admission to trading
content_to_be_reported:
Is A.15 true? If true: N/A, else please Provide: Financial condition of the offeror or
person seeking admission to trading
over the past three years.
This shall be assessed based on a
fair review of the development and
performance of the business of the
offeror or person seeking admission
to trading and of its position for each
year and interim period for which
historical financial information is
required, including the causes of
material changes.
The review shall be a balanced and
comprehensive analysis of the
development and performance of the
business of the offeror or person
seeking admission to trading and of
its position, consistent with the size
and complexity of the business.
The analysis shall include both
financial and, where appropriate,
non-financial Key Performance
Indicators relevant to the business.
The analysis shall, where appropriate,
include references to, and additional
explanations of, amounts reported in
the annual financial statements
(where available), information
regarding unusual or infrequent
events or new developments,
materially affecting the income from
operations and indicate the extent to
which income was so affected,
information concerning capital
resources (both short term and long
term) and an explanation of the
sources and amounts of and a
narrative description of the cash
flows.
[Suggested structure below]
[to fill] started its activities on [to
fill]. The share capital of [to fill] is [to
fill].
[Description of original capital
resources, additional capital
injections and cash flows]
As of December 31, [year], its
financial condition was [to fill], with
a treasury position of [to fill], with
[to fill] debt and [to fill] total assets.
[to fill]’s net operating income… [to
fill].
Full year figures can be found
available on [to fill].

section_name: Part A Information about the offeror or the person seeking admission to trading
content_to_be_reported:
Is A.15 false? If false: N/A, else: Where the offeror or person seeking
admission to trading has not been
established for the past three years,
description of its financial condition
since the date of its registration.
This shall be assessed based on a
fair review of the development and
performance of the business of the
offeror or person seeking admission
to trading and of its position for each
year and interim period for which
historical financial information is
available, including the causes of
material changes.
The review shall be a balanced and
comprehensive analysis of the
development and performance of the
business of the offeror or person
seeking admission to trading and of
its position, consistent with the size
and complexity of the business.

national law, e.g. 254900ARU0VC1WY6GJ71

body, as defined in Article 3(1) point (27) of
Regulation (EU) 2023/1114, of the issuer.

activities and principal markets.

section_name: Part D Information about the crypto-asset project
content_to_be_reported:
A brief description of the crypto-asset project.
For a L1:
[Crypto-asset project] is a [description of the
network] that utilises [consensus algorithm] to
achieve [benefits such as high throughput, low
latency, or enhanced security]. It supports various
applications including [list of applications]. The
network is governed by [description of governing
body], which oversees [list of responsibilities such
as establishing policies, managing treasury,
setting network rules, and maintaining a
transaction history].”
For a token:
“[Token Name] is a token built on [blockchain
networks], designed to enhance the [platform
name] ecosystem by providing users with access
to [list of benefits such as fee reductions, loyalty
rewards, or premium features]. The underlying
networks operate on [consensus mechanism],
ensuring [key advantages such as security,
transparency, and efficiency].
[Token Name] integrates with decentralized
finance (DeFi) protocols and leverages smart
contract capabilities to maintain its functionality
and scalability. Its utility is limited to the [platform
name] ecosystem, where it facilitates [specific use
cases]. Unlike general-purpose cryptocurrencies,
[Token Name] is not a payment token but a
platform-specific asset used exclusively for
accessing services and benefits within [platform
name].
Governance and implementation of [Token Name]
are overseen by [governing entity], with support
from [stakeholders such as strategic partners,
independent developers, or the platform’s
community]. These entities collaborate to manage
the token’s infrastructure and ensure its
sustainable operation.”

field: Details of all natural or legal persons involved in the implementation of the crypto-asset project
section_name: Part D Information about the crypto-asset project
content_to_be_reported:
Tabular format with Name in one column and
address/docimile in the other
Context below:

1. Who are the professional advisors involved in the
implementation of the crypto-asset project?
- Who are the legal advisors?
- Who are the financial advisors?
- Who are the compliance advisors?
- Who are the business consulting advisors?
- Are there any other professional advisors?
2, Who are the technical advisors involved in the
implementation of the crypto-asset project?
- Who are the development team?
- Who are the founder(s)?
- Who are the key developers?
- Who are the financiers?
3. Are there any other key members of the
development team?
- Who are the crypto-asset service providers involved in
the implementation of the crypto-asset project?
- Who provides exchange and trading and related
services?
- Who provides custody services?
- Who provides administrative services?
- Who provides operational services?
4. Are there any other crypto-asset service providers?
- Are there any other persons involved in the
implementation of the crypto-asset project?
- Who are the parties responsible for the administration
or management of the project?
- Are there any other parties with a significant financial
interest in the project as a whole?
For each of these persons, present answers in tabular
format with three columns: NAME, ROLE;
ADDRESS/DOMICILE
form_and_standards: Free alphanumerical text presented in a tabular format

section_name: Part D Information about the crypto-asset project
content_to_be_reported: Indication as to whether the crypto-asset project concerns utility tokens. Expected response: True/False (Yes/No).

field: Key Features of Goods/Services for Utility Token Projects
section_name: Part D Information about the crypto-asset project
content_to_be_reported:
If C.6 is N/A then this is N/A, Otherwise: 
If D.6 is false, this is N/A, otherwise:
Example:
“[Token Name] serves as a [payment/value
voucher/utility] within the [platform name]
ecosystem, granting access to specific functions
and enabling the exchange of value for certain
services. It does not represent ownership, equity,
or participation in [Issuer Name]
Current Key Features:
[Feature 1]: [Brief description of how the token is
used, e.g., loyalty rewards, discounts, access to
premium services].
[Feature 2]: [Further description of functionality,
such as cashback incentives, trading benefits, or
platform rewards].
Future Features:
[Feature 3]: [Planned integrations, expanded
trading capabilities, additional reward
mechanisms, or enhanced token utility].
[Feature 4]: [Additional feature supporting
ecosystem growth and user engagement].
[Token Name] is designed to support the
long-term development of [platform name] by
incentivizing participation, improving access to
services, and expanding its role within the
broader ecosystem.”

section_name: Part D Information about the crypto-asset project
content_to_be_reported:
Information about the crypto-asset project, including the description of the past and future milestones.
Example:
● Past Milestones:
○ [Year-Year]: [Description of research or
development activities].
○ [Date]: [Publication or release of significant
documents or software].
○ [Quarter Year]: [Important events,
partnerships, or achievements].
● Future Milestones:
○ [Quarter Year]: [Description of planned
developments or launches].
○ [Year]: [Long-term goals and strategic plans].

section_name: Part D Information about the crypto-asset project
content_to_be_reported: Where applicable, information about resources, including financial resources, already allocated to the project.

field: Planned use of Collected funds or crypto-assets
section_name: Part D Information about the crypto-asset project
content_to_be_reported:
Guidelines:
1. Introduction:
● Provide a brief statement about the
purpose of the collected funds or
crypto-assets.
● Highlight the importance of strategic
allocation to support the project’s
objectives.
2. Unallocated Funds:
● Specify the amount or percentage of funds
or crypto-assets that remain unallocated.
● Explain that these are reserved for future
strategic uses.
3. Planned Allocations:
● List potential uses for the unallocated
funds or crypto-assets.
● Include specific categories and examples
of how these funds may be utilised.
● Mention that these allocations are subject
to adjustment based on strategic reviews
and market conditions.
4. Detail Specific Allocations:
 Operational Continuity: Funds used for
compensation structures and operational
expenses.
● Outstanding Agreements: Funds allocated
for fulfilling existing agreements such as
SAFTs and TPAs.
● Staking Rewards: Funds reserved to
subsidise staking rewards.
● Ecosystem Development: Funds
designated for independent ecosystem
development organisations or initiatives.
5. Organise Clearly:
● Use bullet points or a list format for clarity.
● Ensure each category is distinct and easy
to understand.

field: Public offering or admission to trading
section_name: Part E Information about the offer to the public of crypto-assets or their admission to trading
content_to_be_reported:
if cryptoAssetSituation = compliance, this is N/A, else:
Check the cryptoAssetSituation variable and respond as follows:
If it indicates admission to trading, write “ATTR”.
If it indicates offer to the public, write “OTPC”.
If it covers both admission to trading and offer to the public, write “BOTH”.
form_and_standards: ATTR or OTPC or BOTH or N/A. These 4 options are the only correct ones

field: Reasons for public offer or admission to trading
section_name: Part E Information about the offer to the public of crypto-assets or their admission to trading
content_to_be_reported:
if cryptoAssetSituation = compliance, this is N/A, else:
The reasons for the offer to the public or for seeking admission to trading, including the planned use of the funds or other crypto-assets collected.
Insert the user’s answer reasonForOffer form questionnaire.
if the user selects in the initial questionnaire that they are looking for an offer or admission to trading or both, they should be asked for their reason for the offer or admission to trading

section_name: Part E Information about the offer to the public of crypto-assets or their admission to trading
Part E Information about the offer to the public of crypto-assets or their admission to trading
content_to_be_reported: CONDITION LOGIC: 1. IF cryptoAssetSituation = "compliance" → SET to "N/A" 2. IF cryptoAssetSituation = "admission" → SET to "N/A" 3. IF cryptoAssetSituation = "offer" → SET to "N/A" 4. ELSE → Generate text using template below. TEMPLATE VARIABLES: [CRYPTO_NAME] = Use DTI if available, otherwise use cryptoAssetName; [OFFEROR_NAME] = Use offerorName from field A.1. TEMPLATE TEXT: "Retail holders who purchase [CRYPTO_NAME] either directly from [OFFEROR_NAME] or from a CASP placing crypto-assets on behalf of [OFFEROR_NAME] shall have a right of withdrawal." CONDITIONAL ADDITION: IF field E.20 = true, THEN append: " until the subscription period end referred to in E.22." SECOND PARAGRAPH: "Retail holders have a period of 14 calendar days within which to withdraw from their agreement to purchase [CRYPTO_NAME] without incurring any fees or costs and without being required to give reasons. The period of withdrawal shall begin from the date of the agreement of the retail holder to purchase [CRYPTO_NAME]. All payments received from a retail holder including, if applicable, any charges, shall be reimbursed without undue delay and in any event no later than 14 days from the date on which [OFFEROR_NAME] or the CASP placing crypto-assets on behalf of [OFFEROR_NAME] is informed of the retail holder's decision to withdraw from the agreement to purchase those crypto-assets. Such reimbursement shall be carried out using the same means of payment as that used by the retail holder for the initial transaction, unless the retail holder expressly agrees otherwise and provided that the retail holder does not incur any fees or costs as a result of such reimbursement."

content_to_be_reported:
if cryptoAssetSituation = compliance, this is N/A, else:
Competent court.

section_name: Part F Information about the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported:
The type of crypto-asset that will be offered to the public or for which admission to trading is sought.
If tokenClassification is:
'ART', Write ART
'EMT', Write EMT
'OTH_UTILITY', Write OTH
'OTH_NON_UTILITY', Write OTH

section_name: Part F Information about the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported:
If all functionalities have been already applied, write "All main crypto asset functionalities have already been deployed" else .A description of the functionality of the crypto-assets being offered or admitted to trading.
Example:
Crypto-Asset Functionality Description
[Token Name] enables users to [perform
specific actions] on the [network name],
such as [list actions/services]. The
token’s utility is designed to ensure
[goal/purpose] across the network.
Planned Application of Functionalities All
functionalities have been deployed,
except [pending functionality], which is
scheduled for [year].”

section_name: Part F Information about the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported:
Information about when the
functionalities of the cryptoassets being
offered or admitted to trading are
planned to apply

section_name: Part F Information about the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported: OTHR

section_name: Part F Information about the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported: Type of submission.
form_and_standards: NEWT = New, MODI = Modify, ERROR = Error, CORR = Correction

section_name: Part F Information about the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported:
A description of the characteristics of the crypto-asset.
Example:
● Characteristics: [Brief description
of unique technological features
and specifications]
● Functionality: [Explanation of
primary uses and roles within the
network]
● Data for Classification:
○ ISO 24165 DTI: [Code]
○ ISO 24165 FFG DTI:
[Code]
○ ISIN: [Code]
● Planned Functionalities:
● [Details on future functionalities
and implementation timeline]
form_and_standards: [ISO 24165 DTI code + ISO 24165 FFG DTI + Free alphanumerical text]

section_name: Part F Information about the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported:
Field to be filled in only if a DTI is not provided in field F.13. Commercial name or trading name of the issuer.
If F.13 has a DTI, should read ""N/A as DTI is provided in F.13""

section_name: Part F Information about the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported: Website of the issuer.

field: Starting date of offer to the public or admission to trading
section_name: Part F Information about the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported: Starting date or, if not available at the time of the notification by the competent authority, the intended starting date of offer to the public or admission to trading.

section_name: Part F Information about the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported: Effective or intended publication date of the crypto-asset white paper or of the modified white paper.

field: Any other services provided by the issuer
section_name: Part F Information about the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported: If section B is applicable read activities written in Field B.11 and write any other services provided by the issuer not covered in the field B.11 and by  Regulation (EU) 2023/1114, with a reference to the applicable Union or national legal acts regulating those services.  If section B is not applicable read activities written in Field A.13 and write any other services provided by the issuer not covered in the field A.13 and by  Regulation (EU) 2023/1114, with a reference to the applicable Union or national legal acts regulating those services.

field: Language or languages of the crypto-asset white paper
section_name: Part F Information about the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported: Language or languages in which the crypto-asset white paper is drafted. When multiple languages have been used, this field shall be reported as many times as necessary.

field: Digital token identifier code used to uniquely identify the crypto-asset or each of the several crypto-assets to which the white paper relates, where available
section_name: Part F Information about the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported: Code used to uniquely identify the crypto-asset or each of the several crypto-assets to which the crypto-asset white paper relates, where available. If not available write N/A

field: Functionally fungible group digital token identifier, where available
section_name: Part F Information about the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported: Code used to uniquely identify the functionally fungible group to which the digital asset belongs (i.e., common to each of the several assets to which the white paper relates). E.g., Code used to identify the white paper ISO 24165 DTI of type 3 (i.e., functionally fungible group), where available. If not available write N/A

section_name: Part F Information about the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported:
Flag indicating the mandatory or voluntary nature of the crypto-asset white paper provided for in Article 4(8) of Regulation (EU) 2023/1114.
If there is a name of at least one natural person in A.12, B.10 (or in the strange case that B.2 is a natural person), ""True""

section_name: Part F Information about the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported: Flag indicating if the submitted white paper contains personal data.

section_name: Part F Information about the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported:
Indication that the issuer is eligible for a Legal Entity Identifier.
If B.7 has a LEI, then this is true.
If A.6 has a LEI, AND B.1 is true, then this is true.
If these are N/a, but B.3 and B.4 have values, then this is true.
If these are N/a, but A.2 and A.3 have values AND B.1 is true, then this is true.

section_name: Part F Information about the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported: Home Member State as defined in Article 3(1), point (33), of Regulation (EU) 2023/1114.
form_and_standards: Closed list of EU Member States

section_name: Part F Information about the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported: Host Member State as defined in Article 3(1), point (34), of Regulation (EU) 2023/1114.
form_and_standards: Closed list of EU Member States

section_name: Part G Information on the rights and obligations attached to the crypto-assets
Part G Information on the rights and obligations attached to the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported:
if hasContractTerms = no AND cryptoAssetSituation != offer this is N/A else:
If G.1 = N/A then this is also N/A else: Procedure and conditions for the exercise of rights.

field: Conditions for modifications of rights and obligations
section_name: Part G Information on the rights and obligations attached to the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported:
if hasContractTerms = no AND cryptoAssetSituation != offer this is N/A else:
If G.1 = N/A then this is also N/A. else: Description of the conditions under which the rights and obligations may be modified

section_name: Part G Information on the rights and obligations attached to the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported:
if futureCryptoOffers = Yes answer questions:
● - What are the minimum target
subscription goals?
● - What are the maximum target
subscription goals?
● - What is the issue price of the
crypto-asset?
● - What are the subscription fees?
● - How many crypto-assets will be offered?
● - Who will the offer be made to (e.g. retail
investors, professional investors, current
holders, etc)?
● - Will any conditions or restrictions attach
to the offer?
● - Will the offer be phased, e.g. will there be
discounts for early purchasers?
● - Who is the CASP in charge of placing the
crypto-assets?
● - When will the offer be made?
● - In which jurisdictions will the offer be
made?
● - What is the planned use of collected
funds

section_name: Part G Information on the rights and obligations attached to the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported: Where applicable, information on the number of crypto-assets retained by the issuer itself.

section_name: Part G Information on the rights and obligations attached to the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported:
Indication as to whether the offer to the public of crypto-assets or their admission to trading concerns utility tokens.
If part E is indeed applicable and D.6 is True, default to True

field: Key features of goods/services of utility tokens
section_name: Part G Information on the rights and obligations attached to the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported: Information about the quality and quantity of goods or services to which the utility tokens give access.

section_name: Part G Information on the rights and obligations attached to the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported: [Where the offers to the public of crypto-assets or their admission to trading concerns utility tokens, information on how utility tokens can be redeemed for goods or services to which they relate]

section_name: Part G Information on the rights and obligations attached to the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported:
Indication whether an admission to trading is sought.
Mapping from questionnaire variable cryptoAssetSituation:
- If 'admission to trading' (ATTR) or BOTH are selected, write TRUE.
- If 'offer to the public' (OTPC) only is selected, write FALSE.
- If 'compliance' only is selected or the variable is not provided, write N/A.
Note: Downstream fields (e.g., G.10) should follow their own applicability rules.
form_and_standards: ‘TRUE’ = sought, ‘FALSE’ = not sought, ‘N/A’ = not applicable (compliance-only or not provided)

section_name: Part G Information on the rights and obligations attached to the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported:
[Where an admission to trading is not sought, information on how and where the crypto-assets can be purchased or sold after the offer to the public]
if admission to trading or both not selected, set G.10 to N/A.

section_name: Part G Information on the rights and obligations attached to the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported:
if hasContractTerms = no AND cryptoAssetSituation != offer this is None else:
[Restrictions on the transferability of the crypto-assets that are being offered or admitted to trading] Suggested structure: 1. Provide a brief statement about the general
rules governing the transfer of the
crypto-assets.
2. Explain any specific network rules that
apply to the transfer of the crypto-assets.
3. Mention any relevant laws and regulations
that govern the transfer, such as AML and
CTF laws.
[Free alphanumerical text]
Our legal partner provided the following advice
Q.1 Is there a smart contract or legal contract or
other set of legal terms provided by the issuer that
govern how token-holders may hold or use the
crypto-asset tokens? (Y/N) If ""Y"", go to Q2.
If ""N"", answer field ""None"".
Q.2 Are there any restrictions on the transferability
of the crypto-assets?
For example, do any of the following restrictions
apply:
● Restrictions on transferring the
crypto-assets to other holders?
● Restrictions on where crypto-assets may
be used to purchase goods or services?
● Time limits on transferring the
crypto-assets?

section_name: Part G Information on the rights and obligations attached to the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported:
Indication as to whether the crypto-asset has protocols for the increase or decrease of its supply in response to changes in demand.
For the OTH whitepaper type, if you do not find enough info to respond confidently, default to False.

section_name: Part G Information on the rights and obligations attached to the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported:
If G.12 is TRUE then [Where the crypto-assets has protocols for the increase or decrease of their supply in response to changes in demand, a description of the functioning of such protocols] Suggested structure:
1. Fixed Supply Overview: State if the total
supply is fixed and provide a brief overview
of the supply schedule.
2. Influencing Factors: Describe the factors
that influence the supply, such as release
schedules, agreements, and reward rates.
3. Role of Governance: Explain how the
governing body or protocol can influence
supply adjustments through decisions on
reward rates or other mechanisms.
If G.12 is false, then field is N/A.

section_name: Part G Information on the rights and obligations attached to the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported: Indication as to whether the crypto-asset has a protection scheme protecting the value of the crypto-asset.

section_name: Part G Information on the rights and obligations attached to the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported: Where the field G.14 is true, a description of the protection schemes protecting the value of the crypto-assets. If G.14 false then this is N/A

section_name: Part G Information on the rights and obligations attached to the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported: Indication as to whether the crypto-asset has a compensation scheme.

section_name: Part G Information on the rights and obligations attached to the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported: Where the field G.16 is true, a description of the compensation schemes. If G.16 is false write N/A

section_name: Part G Information on the rights and obligations attached to the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported:
if hasContractTerms = no AND cryptoAssetSituation != offer this is "There is no written legal agreement between the issuer and the crypto asset holder that sets out the laws that govern the legal relationship between those two parties. In the absence of such an agreement, the laws that govern that relationship will depend on the location of the issuer (to the extent that can be identified) and the given crypto asset holder and characteristic performance of the legal relationship, and any agreed intention of the issuer and crypto asset holder." else:
The law applicable to the crypto-assets.

section_name: Part G Information on the rights and obligations attached to the crypto-assets
content_to_be_reported:
if hasContractTerms = no AND cryptoAssetSituation != offer this is "There is no written legal agreement between the issuer and the crypto asset holder that sets out which jurisdiction's courts will have authority to deal with a dispute between the crypto asset holder and the issuer. In the absence of such an agreement, the laws that competent court will depend on the location of the issuer and the given token-holder and characteristic performance of the legal relationship, and any agreed intention of the issuer and crypto asset holder." else:
Competent court.

section_name: Part H Information on the underlying technology
content_to_be_reported:
If DTI present in F.13 set to N/A else:
[Suggested structure for L1s and L2s] Introduction: ● [Brief overview of the unique DLT and its advantages.] Key Elements: ● Consensus Mechanism: [Description of the consensus algorithm and its uniqueness.] ● Information Propagation: [Explanation of the communication protocols.] Detailed Features: ● Information Spread: [Detail how information spreads among nodes.] ● Consensus Achievement: [Explain the process of achieving consensus within the network.] ● Transaction Structure: [Describe transaction and event structuring and timestamping.] Efficiency and Security: ● [Highlight performance, latency, and security.] ● [Explain integrity and order of transactions.] Node Types: ● Validator Nodes: [Describe their role.] ● Community Nodes: [Mention their role and status.] ● Historical Nodes: [Explain their function and benefits.] Suggested structure for tokens:
[Token Name] operates on [list blockchain networks],
utilizing their unique capabilities to enable secure,
transparent, and efficient transactions.
● [Blockchain 1]: Supports [Token Name] as a
[token standard, e.g., ERC-20], ensuring
compatibility with wallets, smart contracts,
and DeFi applications.
● [Blockchain 2]: Provides [key benefits such
as high throughput, low fees, or enhanced
scalability], facilitating fast and cost-effective
transactions.
● [Blockchain 3]: Enhances [Token Name]’s
functionality through [specific technology,
e.g., zero-knowledge proofs, sidechains, or
improved privacy features], optimizing
performance and interoperability.
By leveraging multiple blockchain networks, [Token
Name] achieves a balance of security, efficiency, and
scalability, ensuring a robust foundation for its use
within the [platform name] ecosystem.

section_name: Part H Information on the underlying technology
content_to_be_reported:
[Information on the protocols and technical standards used, allowing for the holding, storing and transfer of the token] [Suggested structure] Introduction: ● [Brief overview of the protocols and technical standards used.] Distributed Ledger Technology (DLT): ● [Type of DLT]: [Description of the DLT type and its key features and advantages.] Security Standards: ● [Security Protocols]: [Description of the
security protocols and standards ensuring
network robustness.]
Data Handling:
● [Holding, Storing, and Transfer]: [Explanation
of how the protocol manages these
functions.]
● [Technical Standards]: [Mention of any
relevant standards supporting data handling.]

section_name: Part H Information on the underlying technology
content_to_be_reported: [Other information on the technology used allowing for the holding, storing and transfer of asset-referenced tokens, if relevant] [If H1/E1 was left empty, follow its structure]

section_name: Part H Information on the underlying technology
content_to_be_reported:
[Suggested structure] Introduction: ● [Overview of the consensus mechanism and its unique aspects.] Information Propagation: ● Method: [Description of the communication method used by nodes, such as gossip protocols.] ● Efficiency: [Explanation of the efficiency of how information spreads across the network.] Consensus Process: ● Algorithm: [Detail the algorithm or method
used to achieve consensus (e.g., Proof of
Work, Proof of Stake).]
● Transaction Structure: [Describe how
transactions are structured and ordered.]
Data Structures:
● Key Structures: [Explain the key data
structures used, such as linking transactions
or events.]
● Mechanisms: [Detail any special mechanisms
like voting.]
Security and Fairness:
● Protocols: [Highlight security protocols
protecting against malicious activities.]
● Ordering: [Explain how transaction ordering is
achieved.]
Node Roles and Responsibilities:
● Node Types: [Outline the different types of
nodes and their roles (e.g., validator nodes,
leader nodes).]
● Responsibilities: [Explain the responsibilities
of each node type.]
Additional Features:
● Optimisations: [Mention additional features
that enhance the consensus mechanism,
such as sharding.]
● Benefits: [Explain how these features improve
the systems performance.]

section_name: Part H Information on the underlying technology
content_to_be_reported: Information on incentive mechanisms to secure transactions and any fees applicable.

section_name: Part H Information on the underlying technology
content_to_be_reported:
[Indication as to whether the crypto-assets are issued, transferred and stored using distributed ledger technology that is operated by the issuer, the
offeror or a third-party acting on their behalf]
form_and_standards: ‘TRUE’ = Yes, DLT operated by the issuer or a third-party acting on the issuer’s behalf. ‘FALSE’ = No, DLT not operated by the issuer or a third-party acting on the issuer’s behalf.

section_name: Part H Information on the underlying technology
content_to_be_reported:
[Detailed description of the functioning of such distributed ledger technology] ● Introduction: [Briefly introduce the DLT and its main capabilities.] ● Core Functionalities: ○ Asset Management: Manage digital assets: creation, transfer, lifecycle. ○ Data Integrity: Ensure data integrity and immutability through cryptographic methods. ● Advanced Functionalities: ○ Smart Contract creation and execution. ○ Identity Management: Support of digital identities. ● Interoperability: How it enables the interaction with other networks and systems. ● User Interaction: ○ User Interfaces. ○ Developer Tools. ● Performance and Scalability: ○ Efficiency Measures: Features for optimising transaction speed and performance. ○ Scalability Solutions: Methods for scaling the network to support growth.
If H.6 is false, set to N/A

section_name: Part H Information on the underlying technology
content_to_be_reported: [Indication as to whether an audit of the technology used was conducted]

section_name: Part H Information on the underlying technology
content_to_be_reported:
If H.8 is true: [Information on the audit outcome of the technology used] ● Introduction: [Provide a brief overview of the audit, including its purpose and scope, and highlight the significance of the audit outcome.] ● Audit Details ● Methodology: The audit employed [tools/methods], such as formal verification using [specific tool or framework]. ● Audit Findings: ○ The audit confirmed key findings, such as [key findings]. ○ Received certifications or confirmations. ● Significance: ● Independent Review: ○ The audit was conducted by [auditors/entities], known for their expertise in [relevant field]. ○ Led by [names/titles]. ● Scope of Review: ○ The review included [components/services], assessing the security, stability, and correctness of the technology. ● Outcome Summary:
If H.8 is false, set to N/A

section_name: Part I Information on the risks
content_to_be_reported: A description of the risks associated with the offer to the public of crypto-assets or their admission to trading] ● [Briefly introduce the context, mentioning if the offer to the public has already passed and the relevance of trading risks.] ● Irreversibility of Transactions: ○ Describe if payments can be reversed, deleted, or frozen by the network or governing body. ○ Describe if payments made under coercion, deceit, mistakes, or unlawfully are irreversible, similar to physical cash. ○ Describe if courts can mandate compensatory transactions, but lack the technical means to enforce such orders without private keys. ● Standard Trading Risks: ○ Pricing Volatility: Crypto-asset prices are susceptible to significant fluctuations due to market dynamics. ● Liquidity Conditions: Insufficient liquidity on exchanges or trading platforms could lead to large price swings, impacting the ability to sell assets without significant price concessions. ● Market Demand: There is a risk that the DLT may not achieve expected levels of market adoption, impacting the utility and value of the asset. ● Impact of Large Transactions: Large transactions by major holders could significantly influence the price, leading to potential manipulation or unintended price destabilisation. OTH S301b F.3 ART ● Broader Economic Conditions: Wider economic downturns or financial market instability could lead to decreased investment in crypto-assets, adversely affecting their price and liquidity. ● Regulatory Changes: Evolving regulatory landscapes may impact the offer and trading of crypto-assets. ● Mitigation Strategies: Use of intermediaries, particularly regulated ones with robust customer support, can help mitigate some of these risks, although it reintroduces conventional risks associated with dependence on third parties.

section_name: Part I Information on the risks
content_to_be_reported: [A description of the risks associated with the issuer, if different from the offeror or person seeking admission to trading, having regard to risks related to the issuers financial situation, risks related to the issuers business activities and industry, legal and regulatory risk, internal control risk, environmental, social and governance risks] Treasury Risks: ● Describe the reliance on financial reserves or treasury to support operations. ● Highlight the risks related to insufficient revenue generation or unexpected financial shortfalls. Internal Efficiency and Control Risks: ● Explain the importance of efficient allocation of resources for sustainable development. ● Discuss potential risks from mismanagement or inefficient use of resources. Operational Integrity: ● Highlight the need for strong internal controls to ensure operational integrity. ● Mention risks associated with weaknesses in fraud prevention, error management, and maintaining operational efficiency. OTH S302b F.1 EMT S302c F.2 ART Governance Practices: ● Discuss the significance of robust governance practices. ● Address risks related to transparency, decision-making efficiency, and the balance between centralisation and decentralisation. Partnership Dependencies: ● Describe the reliance on key partnerships for operational and strategic goals. Be specific about who these partners are. ● Highlight the risks associated with disruptions in these partnerships or failure to meet collaborative objectives. Regulatory Risks: ● Explain the need to navigate complex regulatory environments. ● Discuss risks related to non-compliance, changes in legislation, and potential legal challenges.

section_name: Part I Information on the risks
content_to_be_reported: Introduction: [Provide a brief overview of the key risks associated with holding and trading crypto-assets.] ● Core Risks: ○ Volatility Risks: Describe susceptibility to significant price fluctuations due to market dynamics. ○ Liquidity Risks: Explain potential challenges in buying or selling assets without significant price concessions due to insufficient market liquidity. ○ Market Manipulation Risks: Highlight risks of price manipulation by large holders or coordinated activities. ○ Market Risks: Discuss broader market risks that could affect the value and stability of the asset. OTH S303b F.2 Token-related risks EMT S303c F.4 ART ○ Irreversibility Risks: Explain risks associated with the irreversibility of transactions on the blockchain. ● Specific Holder Risks: ○ Private Key Management Risks: Describe risks related to the loss, theft, or improper management of private keys, which are essential for accessing assets. ○ Custodial Risks: Explain risks associated with relying on third-party custodians for private key management, including potential losses due to custodial failures ○ Privacy Risks: Highlight risks related to the public recording of transactions on the blockchain, which could expose user activities and potentially link pseudonymous addresses to real-world identities.

section_name: Part I Information on the risks
content_to_be_reported: Introduction: [Provide a brief overview of the risks associated with the implementation of the crypto-asset project.] ● Development and Operational Risks: ○ Technical Delays and Overruns: Describe risks of delays in development timelines or budget overruns due to unforeseen technical challenges, complexity in technology integration, or underestimation of resource requirements. ○ Quality Assurance: Explain potential issues with software bugs, security vulnerabilities, or performance shortfalls that could emerge despite testing. ● Dependency Risks: ○ Dependency on Third Parties: Highlight risks related to reliance on external partners or service providers, including potential delays and risks introduced by third parties’ OTH independence in setting their own communication policies. ● Adoption and Community Risks: ○ Adoption by Users: Describe the risk that new features or updates may not be adopted by users due to lack of perceived benefits, complexity in transition, or user resistance to change. ○ Community Support: Explain risks related to lack of engagement or alignment with the developer community, which can result in slower technological progress and potential negative publicity. ● Market and Human Resources Risks: ○ Market Penetration: Discuss challenges in penetrating target markets due to competition, lack of market readiness, or ineffective marketing strategies. ○ Human Resources: Highlight risks related to dependency on key personnel and maintaining a skilled workforce, including the potential impact of losing key developers or executives.

section_name: Part I Information on the risks
content_to_be_reported: ● Introduction: [Provide a brief overview of the technology-related risks associated with the crypto-asset project.] ● Specific Technology-Related Risks: ○ Majority Attacks: Describe the risk of network disruption or manipulation if a majority of the networks computational power or stake is compromised. ○ Network Proxy and Service Attacks: Highlight vulnerabilities related to network proxies and specific services, such as potential exploits in smart contracts or APIs. OTH S305b F.3 EMT S305c F.6 ART ○ Code Quality and Bugs: Explain the risk of software bugs and defects in new releases or updates that could compromise system functionality, security, and user trust. ○ Protocol Updates: Discuss risks associated with updates to the protocol, including the potential for introducing vulnerabilities or disruptions. ○ Cryptographic Risks: Describe the risks associated with current cryptographic methods, including the potential future threat posed by quantum computing. ○ Scalability Risks: Highlight the risks and technical challenges associated with scaling the network, such as implementing sharding or other scaling solutions. ○ Interoperability Risks: Discuss the importance of interoperability with other blockchain projects and traditional systems, and the risks of delays in achieving it. ○ Other Attacks: Describe other conceivable attacks, such as denial-of-service attacks or network partitioning, which could disrupt network communication.

section_name: Part I Information on the risks
content_to_be_reported: Should describe measures that match I.1 to I.6   Introduction: [Provide a brief overview of the mitigation measures in place to address various risks associated with the crypto-asset project.] Offer-Related Risks: ● Market Education: Educate holders and prospective holders about market risks. ● Treasury Management: Implement a carefully designed Treasury Allocation Plan to avoid market disruption. ● Regulatory Engagement: Engage with regulators to minimise negative impacts from regulatory changes. ● Issuer-Related Risks: OTH S306b F.4 EMT S306c F.7 ART ○ Financial Reserves: Maintain robust financial reserves and employ stringent budgeting practices. ○ Internal Audits: Conduct regular internal audits to enhance operational controls. ○ Transparency: Commit to transparency in decision-making processes and publish governance information. ● Decentralisation: Increase network decentralisation by expanding node numbers and distributing governance roles. ● Partnership Diversification: Engage with a variety of partners to spread dependency risks. ● Regulatory Compliance: Proactively engage with regulatory developments and ensure compliance. ● Operational Security: Follow strict operational security procedures for key management. Crypto-Assets-Related Risks: ● Market Education: Educate holders about best practices without influencing asset prices. Project Implementation-Related Risks: ● Security Assessments: Conduct continuous security assessments and updates. ● Community Engagement: Maintain active channels for community interaction and feedback. ● Developer Encouragement: Encourage developer activity and proposals through partnerships. ● Dependency Reduction: Work actively to reduce dependencies on particular partners. ● Human Resources: Uphold high standards in human resources and operational efficiency. Technology-Related Risks: ● Vetting and Decentralisation: Vet new council members and ensure network conditions prevent majority attacks. ● Security Audits: Conduct regular internal security audits to identify vulnerabilities. ● Code Review Process: Implement a rigorous code review process to catch potential bugs. ● Bug Bounty Programme: Maintain an active bug bounty programme to incentivise external security assessments. ● Post-Quantum Cryptography: Adhere to advanced cryptographic standards and prepare for post-quantum cryptography. ● Interoperability and Scalability: Facilitate interoperability and make technical advancements for sharding and post-quantum security

content_to_be_reported: Data provided by the MiCA Crypto Alliance as a third party, with no deviations from the calculation guidance of Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2025/422, Article 6(5). Full methodology available at: www.micacryptoalliance.com/methodologies

section_name: Part J: Information on the sustainability indicators in relation to adverse impact on the climate and other environment-related adverse impacts
content_to_be_reported: Data provided by the MiCA Crypto Alliance as a third party, with no deviations from the calculation guidance of Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2025/422, Article 6(5). As the asset runs on a decentralised network, estimates on individual node weight, hazardous components and deprecation rate are used. Full methodology available at: www.micacryptoalliance.com/methodologies

section_name: Part J: Information on the sustainability indicators in relation to adverse impact on the climate and other environment-related adverse impacts
content_to_be_reported: Data provided by the MiCA Crypto Alliance as a third party, with no deviations from the calculation guidance of Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2025/422, Article 6(5). Usage of natural resources is approximated through land use metrics. Land use, water use and water recycling are calculated based on energy mix-specific estimates of purchased electricity land intensity, purchased electricity water intensity, and water recycling rates. Full methodology available at: www.micacryptoalliance.com/methodologies
